consequence: Missense variant in VCP N-domain disrupting cofactor interaction and ATPase regulation
text_chunk: VCP R155H is the most common pathogenic variant in valosin-containing protein, accounting for approximately 50% of IBMPFD cases. This missense substitution in the N-domain alters cofactor binding and disrupts the coordination between ATPase activity and substrate processing, leading to accumulation of ubiquitinated protein aggregates in muscle, bone, and brain tissue.
disease_association: Inclusion body myopathy with Paget disease and frontotemporal dementia (IBMPFD)

consequence: Missense variant in VCP N-D1 linker region impairing hexamer conformational dynamics
text_chunk: VCP R191Q disrupts the interface between the N-domain and D1 ATPase ring, altering hexamer conformational cycling required for substrate unfolding. Carriers present with progressive inclusion body myopathy and frontotemporal dementia, typically with onset in the fourth to fifth decade. This variant demonstrates elevated basal ATPase activity and impaired autophagosome maturation in patient-derived fibroblasts.
disease_association: Frontotemporal dementia, inclusion body myopathy

consequence: Missense variant near D1 ATPase domain affecting substrate processing fidelity
text_chunk: VCP A232E is a pathogenic variant located at the N-D1 domain boundary that impairs the coupling between ATP hydrolysis and mechanical work. Functional studies show defective endosomal trafficking and accumulation of TDP-43-positive inclusions. Clinical presentation includes ALS-like motor neuron degeneration and frontotemporal dementia with variable Paget disease of bone penetrance.
disease_association: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, frontotemporal dementia, Paget disease of bone

consequence: Frameshift variant producing truncated BRCA1 protein lacking BRCT domains
text_chunk: BRCA1 5382insC is an Ashkenazi Jewish founder mutation with carrier frequency of approximately 1%. The frameshift eliminates the C-terminal BRCT domains essential for DNA damage response signaling and homologous recombination repair. Carriers have 60-80% lifetime breast cancer risk and 20-40% ovarian cancer risk. This variant is a strong indication for PARP inhibitor eligibility in metastatic disease.
disease_association: Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome

consequence: Frameshift variant disrupting BRCA2 DNA-binding domain and RAD51 interaction
text_chunk: BRCA2 6174delT is an Ashkenazi Jewish founder mutation carried by approximately 1.5% of this population. The frameshift disrupts the C-terminal DNA-binding domain and RAD51 interaction sites critical for homologous recombination repair. Male carriers have elevated prostate and breast cancer risk. Tumors with this variant exhibit BRCAness phenotype with sensitivity to platinum chemotherapy and PARP inhibitors.
disease_association: Hereditary breast cancer, ovarian cancer, prostate cancer, pancreatic cancer

consequence: Hotspot missense variant abolishing DNA-binding and conferring gain-of-function oncogenic activity
text_chunk: TP53 R175H is the most frequent somatic TP53 mutation across all cancer types and a germline variant in Li-Fraumeni syndrome. This structural mutant completely loses wild-type tumor suppressor function while acquiring gain-of-function properties including enhanced cell migration, drug resistance, and genomic instability. R175H stabilizes the mutant p53 protein, enabling dominant-negative effects on residual wild-type p53.
disease_association: Li-Fraumeni syndrome, multiple cancer types

consequence: Contact mutant destroying direct DNA-binding at p53 response elements
text_chunk: TP53 R248W is a DNA-contact mutant that directly abolishes p53 binding to target gene promoters. Unlike structural mutants, R248W retains partial protein folding but cannot engage consensus DNA sequences. This variant is highly penetrant in Li-Fraumeni syndrome and is among the most common somatic TP53 alterations in serous ovarian carcinoma, colorectal cancer, and head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.
disease_association: Li-Fraumeni syndrome, serous ovarian carcinoma, colorectal cancer

consequence: Missense variant reducing MTHFR regulatory domain activity by 15-30%
text_chunk: MTHFR A1298C (rs1801131) produces a glutamate-to-alanine substitution in the regulatory domain. Homozygous CC carriers have 15-30% reduced enzyme activity, less severe than C677T but clinically relevant when compound heterozygous (677CT/1298AC). The combination impairs folate metabolism sufficiently to elevate homocysteine and may increase neural tube defect risk. Population frequency is approximately 30% for the C allele in Europeans.
disease_association: Hyperhomocysteinemia, neural tube defects, compound MTHFR deficiency

consequence: Homozygous APOE4 genotype with severely impaired amyloid-beta clearance and lipid metabolism
text_chunk: APOE E4/E4 homozygosity confers 12-15 fold increased risk of Alzheimer disease with typical onset in the sixth decade. The E4 isoform binds LDL receptors with reduced affinity, impairing cerebral amyloid-beta clearance and cholesterol redistribution in the CNS. E4/E4 carriers also show accelerated tau pathology, blood-brain barrier dysfunction, and neuroinflammation. LDL cholesterol is elevated by 20-40 mg/dL compared to E3/E3 carriers.
disease_association: Alzheimer disease, cardiovascular disease, cerebral amyloid angiopathy

consequence: Splice-site variant abolishing CYP2D6 enzyme activity (null allele)
text_chunk: CYP2D6*4 is the most common null allele in European populations (frequency ~20-25%). The G>A splice defect at intron 3/exon 4 boundary produces a non-functional enzyme. Homozygous *4/*4 carriers are poor metabolizers affecting codeine (no analgesia due to absent morphine conversion), tamoxifen (reduced endoxifen levels), and many antidepressants. CPIC Level 1A guidelines recommend alternative agents for poor metabolizers.
disease_association: Pharmacogenomic poor metabolizer phenotype

consequence: Splice-site variant producing non-functional CYP2C19 enzyme
text_chunk: CYP2C19*2 is the most prevalent loss-of-function allele globally, with frequencies of 15% in Europeans and 30% in East Asians. The splice defect eliminates enzyme activity. Poor metabolizers (*2/*2) cannot activate clopidogrel, leading to inadequate platelet inhibition and increased cardiovascular event risk post-stenting. CPIC guidelines recommend prasugrel or ticagrelor as alternatives for poor and intermediate metabolizers.
disease_association: Clopidogrel resistance, increased stent thrombosis risk

consequence: Promoter variant increasing CYP2C19 transcription and enzyme activity
text_chunk: CYP2C19*17 is a gain-of-function promoter variant that increases enzyme expression 1.5-2 fold. Ultra-rapid metabolizers (*17/*17, frequency ~5% Europeans) may experience subtherapeutic levels of proton pump inhibitors, voriconazole, and certain SSRIs due to accelerated drug clearance. Conversely, clopidogrel activation is enhanced, potentially increasing bleeding risk. This variant is pharmacogenomically significant despite benign disease classification.
disease_association: Ultra-rapid drug metabolism, increased bleeding risk with clopidogrel

consequence: Loss of hepatic lipase activity causing triglyceride accumulation in lipid droplets
text_chunk: PNPLA3 I148M is the strongest genetic determinant of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease. The G allele frequency reaches 50% in Hispanic populations. GG homozygotes have 3-fold increased hepatic fat content, 5-fold NASH risk, and significantly elevated ALT. The variant abolishes lipid droplet triglyceride hydrolase activity, and the mutant protein resists ubiquitin-mediated degradation, accumulating on lipid droplets and sequestering lipase cofactors.
disease_association: NAFLD, NASH, hepatic fibrosis, hepatocellular carcinoma

consequence: Disrupted HFE protein folding preventing surface expression and hepcidin regulation
text_chunk: HFE C282Y homozygosity is the primary cause of hereditary hemochromatosis in Northern European populations (prevalence ~0.5%). The cysteine-to-tyrosine substitution disrupts a disulfide bond in the alpha-3 domain, preventing HFE association with beta-2-microglobulin and cell surface expression. Without functional HFE, hepcidin production is insufficient, leading to unregulated intestinal iron absorption and progressive multi-organ iron deposition.
disease_association: Hereditary hemochromatosis, iron overload, liver cirrhosis, cardiomyopathy

consequence: Missense variant with mild effect on HFE-transferrin receptor interaction
text_chunk: HFE H63D is a common polymorphism (allele frequency ~15% in Europeans) with mild effects on iron homeostasis. H63D homozygotes rarely develop clinical hemochromatosis, but compound heterozygosity with C282Y (C282Y/H63D) confers moderate iron overload risk in approximately 1-2% of compound heterozygotes. H63D alone may contribute to mildly elevated ferritin and transferrin saturation without clinical iron overload in most carriers.
disease_association: Mild iron overload, elevated ferritin (compound heterozygotes)

consequence: Thermolabile enzyme with 30-70% reduced methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase activity
text_chunk: MTHFR C677T produces a thermolabile enzyme variant. TT homozygotes (frequency ~10-12% in Europeans, up to 25% in some Hispanic populations) have 70% reduced activity, causing impaired conversion of 5,10-methyleneTHF to 5-methylTHF. This impairs homocysteine remethylation to methionine, elevating plasma homocysteine by 20-40%. Methylfolate supplementation bypasses the enzymatic block and normalizes homocysteine in most TT carriers.
disease_association: Hyperhomocysteinemia, neural tube defects, cardiovascular disease

consequence: Impaired hepatic remnant lipoprotein clearance with paradoxically low LDL
text_chunk: APOE E2/E2 homozygosity (frequency ~1%) produces an isoform with markedly reduced LDL receptor binding (2% of normal). While most E2/E2 carriers have very low LDL cholesterol, approximately 5-10% develop type III hyperlipoproteinemia with accumulation of remnant lipoproteins, palmar xanthomas, and accelerated atherosclerosis. Metabolic triggers such as obesity, diabetes, or hypothyroidism unmask the dyslipidemia phenotype in susceptible carriers.
disease_association: Type III hyperlipoproteinemia, dysbetalipoproteinemia, premature atherosclerosis

consequence: Reduced CYP2D6 enzyme stability and activity (decreased function allele)
text_chunk: CYP2D6*10 is the most common decreased-function allele in East Asian populations (frequency 40-50%). The Pro34Ser substitution destabilizes the protein, reducing enzyme activity to approximately 25-50% of normal. *10/*10 homozygotes are classified as intermediate metabolizers, requiring dose reductions for CYP2D6 substrates including tamoxifen, codeine, and tricyclic antidepressants. CPIC guidelines provide specific dosing adjustments.
disease_association: Intermediate drug metabolism, reduced tamoxifen efficacy

consequence: Splice-site variant completely abolishing dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase activity
text_chunk: DPYD*2A is a splice-site variant that eliminates exon 14, producing a non-functional DPD enzyme. Heterozygous carriers (frequency ~1-2% Europeans) have 50% reduced 5-fluorouracil clearance and are at high risk for severe, potentially fatal fluoropyrimidine toxicity including grade 4 neutropenia, mucositis, and hand-foot syndrome. CPIC Level 1A guidelines mandate preemptive DPYD genotyping before fluoropyrimidine administration with 50% dose reduction for carriers.
disease_association: Severe fluoropyrimidine toxicity, DPD deficiency

consequence: Reduced hepatic uptake transporter function increasing statin plasma exposure
text_chunk: SLCO1B1*5 reduces hepatic statin uptake by 30-70%, increasing plasma simvastatin acid exposure and myopathy risk. The SEARCH trial demonstrated that rs4149056 CC homozygotes have 17-fold increased risk of simvastatin-induced myopathy at 80mg dose. CPIC guidelines recommend avoiding simvastatin doses above 20mg in CC carriers and considering alternative statins (rosuvastatin, pravastatin) with lower SLCO1B1 dependence for hepatic uptake.
disease_association: Statin-induced myopathy, rhabdomyolysis risk

consequence: Two missense variants causing rapid protein degradation and absent TPMT activity
text_chunk: TPMT*3A carries two missense variants (Ala154Thr and Tyr240Cys) that cause protein misfolding and accelerated proteasomal degradation. Homozygous *3A/*3A individuals (frequency ~0.3%) have absent TPMT activity and cannot tolerate standard thiopurine doses (azathioprine, mercaptopurine). Without dose reduction to 10% of standard, these patients develop life-threatening myelosuppression. CPIC Level 1A guidelines require preemptive genotyping.
disease_association: Thiopurine toxicity, myelosuppression, DPD-like drug sensitivity

consequence: Activated protein C resistance due to abolished Factor Va cleavage site
text_chunk: Factor V Leiden (R506Q) is the most common inherited thrombophilia, carried by 5% of Europeans. The arginine-to-glutamine substitution eliminates the primary activated protein C (APC) cleavage site on Factor Va, causing APC resistance and a prothrombotic state. Heterozygotes have 5-7 fold increased VTE risk; homozygotes have 50-80 fold risk. Oral contraceptives synergistically increase risk to 35-fold in heterozygotes.
disease_association: Venous thromboembolism, deep vein thrombosis, pulmonary embolism

consequence: Loss-of-function variant reducing PCSK9-mediated LDL receptor degradation
text_chunk: PCSK9 R46L is a loss-of-function variant (frequency ~3% Europeans) that reduces circulating PCSK9, increases hepatic LDL receptor density, and lowers LDL cholesterol by 15-28 mg/dL. Long-term follow-up in the ARIC study demonstrated 88% reduction in coronary heart disease events in carriers. This natural experiment validated PCSK9 as a drug target, leading to development of evolocumab and alirocumab antibody therapies.
disease_association: Reduced cardiovascular disease risk, familial hypocholesterolemia (protective)

consequence: Reduced glucocerebrosidase activity with residual enzyme function
text_chunk: GBA1 N370S is the most common Gaucher disease variant in Ashkenazi Jewish populations (carrier frequency ~6%). Homozygotes develop type 1 Gaucher disease with hepatosplenomegaly and bone disease but without neurological involvement. Heterozygous carriers have 5-fold increased risk of Parkinson disease due to impaired lysosomal alpha-synuclein degradation. This variant connects lysosomal storage disorders to neurodegenerative disease through shared proteostasis pathways.
disease_association: Gaucher disease type 1, increased Parkinson disease risk

consequence: Deletion causing CFTR protein misfolding, ER retention, and absent chloride channel function
text_chunk: CFTR F508del is the most common cystic fibrosis variant worldwide, present on approximately 70% of CF chromosomes. The phenylalanine deletion causes CFTR protein misfolding, retention in the endoplasmic reticulum, and proteasomal degradation. Elexacaftor-tezacaftor-ivacaftor (Trikafta) corrects F508del folding defect and potentiates residual channel function, producing dramatic clinical improvement in lung function and quality of life.
disease_association: Cystic fibrosis, CFTR-related metabolic syndrome, congenital bilateral absence of vas deferens

consequence: Misfolded alpha-1-antitrypsin polymerizes in hepatocyte ER causing toxic gain-of-function
text_chunk: SERPINA1 Z allele (E342K) causes alpha-1-antitrypsin deficiency, the most common genetic cause of liver disease in children and emphysema in adults. ZZ homozygotes (frequency ~1/3000 Northern Europeans) have 10-15% of normal serum AAT. The Z protein polymerizes within hepatocyte ER, causing liver injury through toxic gain-of-function, while circulating AAT deficiency allows unopposed neutrophil elastase destruction of lung parenchyma.
disease_association: Alpha-1-antitrypsin deficiency, emphysema, liver cirrhosis, hepatocellular carcinoma

consequence: Splice-site disruption causing exon 7 skipping and non-functional LDL receptor
text_chunk: LDLR splice donor variant c.1060+1G>A disrupts the intron 7 splice site, causing exon skipping and a truncated, non-functional LDL receptor. Heterozygous carriers develop familial hypercholesterolemia with LDL cholesterol 190-400 mg/dL and premature coronary artery disease. Over 2,000 pathogenic LDLR variants have been identified, making molecular diagnosis essential for cascade screening and early aggressive lipid-lowering therapy.
disease_association: Familial hypercholesterolemia, premature coronary artery disease

consequence: Increased CYP2D6 gene copy number producing excess enzyme and ultra-rapid metabolism
text_chunk: CYP2D6 gene duplication produces ultra-rapid metabolizers (frequency 1-10% depending on population, highest in East Africa and Middle East). These individuals have accelerated metabolism of codeine to morphine, risking respiratory depression and death, particularly in breastfed neonates of ultra-rapid metabolizer mothers. Conversely, tamoxifen shows enhanced efficacy. CPIC guidelines contraindicate codeine and recommend alternative analgesics.
disease_association: Ultra-rapid drug metabolism, codeine toxicity, neonatal opioid toxicity

consequence: Reduced UGT1A1 promoter activity causing decreased bilirubin glucuronidation
text_chunk: UGT1A1*28 is a TA repeat promoter variant (7 repeats vs. normal 6) reducing UGT1A1 expression by approximately 70%. Homozygous *28/*28 (frequency ~10% Europeans) causes Gilbert syndrome with intermittent unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia. Critically, reduced UGT1A1 activity impairs irinotecan (SN-38) glucuronidation, increasing severe neutropenia and diarrhea risk. FDA labeling recommends reduced irinotecan dosing for *28/*28 patients.
disease_association: Gilbert syndrome, irinotecan toxicity, unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia

consequence: Missense variant in N-domain alpha-helix altering UBXD1 cofactor binding interface
text_chunk: VCP R159H is a rare pathogenic variant in the N-domain that disrupts interaction with the UBXD1 cofactor required for endolysosomal sorting. Patient cells show impaired autophagosome-lysosome fusion and accumulation of ubiquitin-positive aggregates. Clinical presentation includes late-onset inclusion body myopathy with variable penetrance for frontotemporal dementia and Paget disease. This variant is a candidate for emerging VCP-targeted small molecule correctors.
disease_association: Inclusion body myopathy, frontotemporal dementia, VCP-related multisystem proteinopathy
